notes: SYCL is a Khronos Group royalty-free C++ standard for heterogeneous/accelerator (GPU) computing, central to Intel oneAPI and CUDA-to-SYCL porting. Pronounced SICK-ul (/ˈsɪkəl/) — a deliberate homophone of the farming tool "sickle" — with stress on the first syllable. Non-native devs commonly spell it out "S-Y-C-L" or say "SY-cull" (/ˈsaɪkəl/), missing that the two-syllable name reads like a real English word. Originally "System-wide Compute Language"; since 2020 the creators state it is a name, not an acronym. Pronunciation documented on Wikipedia; no creator/official pronunciation quote located, so labeled community-consensus.
